lineage.summary: The pinned General Corpus XML is a normalized copy of the recovered U-M encoding, delivered with its complete raw package and 816-TIFF edition facsimile. The TIFFs reproduce Horstmann's 1892 Part I and Furnivall's 1901 Part II; those editions assemble 95 poems in 93 editorial units through direct manuscript transcription, collation, supplied passages, and print-mediated comparison. The graph keeps all 95 entities, 106 relations, 91 reviewed paths, access routes, local-copy ownership, and rights layers distinct.

catalogingSubject.scope_note: The electronic selection delimited by the pinned U-M XML: two printed parts issued in 1892 and 1901, containing 95 intellectual poems arranged as 93 numbered/count-once editorial units, plus wrappers, parallel comparison streams, sidenotes, Various Readings, and editorial apparatus. It is not one medieval work, an authorial collection, the entire Vernon manuscript, or a claim that every reading is Vernon based.

summaries.value: An electronic edited selection of 95 Middle English verse poems arranged as 93 editorial units in Carl Horstmann's 1892 Part I and Frederick J. Furnivall's 1901 Part II. Vernon is the principal but not exclusive witness: the editions also print parallel witness texts, supplied passages, earlier-edition readings, collations, sidenotes, Various Readings, and an Appendix. The collection ranges across prayer, liturgical and devotional verse, moral instruction, narrative, debate, satire, and historical polemic.

Electronic edited selection of 95 intellectual poems arranged as 93 numbered/count-once editorial units. Item XL contains two explicit complete poems, and Item XXXVIII embeds the distinct complete 1,247-line Myrour adaptation. The hierarchy has 93 count-bearing editorial units, while item_count and structured extent use the 95-intellectual-poem axis: Items XXXVIII and XL are aggregate parents with item_count=2, and their three overlapping detail children remain null to prevent double counting.
